name: codegen-2
documentation:
# codegen
Meta-programming

methods.name: codegen.models.var.VarScope.from_ast_id
documentation: Get the scope of a variable as if it is assigned to the given AST

class_variables.name: codegen.models.var.VarScope.ast
documentation: the ast that has a child, which is an assign statement, that the first time the variable is defined

class_variables.name: codegen.models.var.VarScope.child_index_start
documentation: the index of the child (assign statement)

classes.name: codegen.models.var.DeferredVar
documentation: Containings the information to create a variable

methods.name: codegen.models.program.Program.get_var
documentation: De-reference a variable from the memory that is available at the provided AST

methods.name: codegen.models.program.VarRegisters.register
documentation:
Register a new variable with the given name, key, then return the register id.

The scope of the variable is created from the given ast.

methods.name: codegen.models.program.VarRegisters.find
documentation:
Find the most specific register by name, key that is available in the given ast. If
there are multiple matches, the most specific register is the one with the largest depth.

class_variables.name: codegen.models.statement.ExceptionStatement.expr
documentation: we rely on special exception expr

methods.name: codegen.models.ast.AST.__call__
documentation: Allow to build the graph hierarchically

methods.name: codegen.models.ast.AST.func
documentation: Define a function. The input variables are deferred vars as they are created for this function (i.e., must not be predefined prior to this function)

methods.name: codegen.models.ast.AST.assign
documentation: When we assign the variable, we accept that either you create/declare the variable for the first time or reassign it

methods.name: codegen.models.ast.AST.for_loop
documentation: When we construct a for-loop, the item (or var) is always declared. We don't allow reassigning the variable in the for-loop.

methods.name: codegen.models.ast.AST.update_recursively
documentation:
Recursively updating the ast. It takes a function that works on the current tree and a context, returns a tuple of
(new_tree, new_context, stop). This function returns the last AST that is updated.

methods.name: codegen.models.ast.AST.to_python
documentation: Convert the AST to python code

methods.name: codegen.models.ast.AST.has_statement_between_ast
documentation: Check if there is a statement of the given type the current AST and the end_ast (exclusive)

methods.name: codegen.models.ast.AST.find_ast_to
documentation: Iterate through ASTs that lead to the AST with the given id (inclusive)

methods.name: codegen.models.ast.AST.find_ast
documentation: Find the AST with the given id

methods.name: codegen.models.ast.AST.next_child_id
documentation: Get ID for the next child of this AST

methods.name: codegen.models.ast.AST.next_grandchild_id
documentation: Get ID for the next grandchild of this AST

methods.name: codegen.models.ast.AST.next_var_scope
documentation: Get a scope for the next variable that will be have if it is assigned to this AST
